samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH">Data width of FIFO.</parameter>
        <parameter id="DEPTH">Depth of FIFO.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    <para>1. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule.</para>
    <para>2. Use the first 4 bits of the read/write pointer as the address of the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>3. Use the lower 3 bits of the read/write pointer as the address of the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>4. Use the lower 3 bits of the read pointer to read data from the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>5. Use the lower 3 bits of the write pointer to write data to the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>6. Use the 4-bit write pointer as the write address.</para>
    <para>7. Use the 4-bit read pointer as the read address.</para>
    <para>8. The write enable signal is active when the FIFO is not full.</para>
    <para>9. The read enable signal is active when the FIFO is not empty.</para>
    <para>10. The write full signal is active when the write pointer is one cycle ahead of the read pointer.</para>
    <para>11. The read empty signal is active when the read pointer is equal to the write pointer.</para>
    </implementation>
</module>
